steps.title: Translate the relationship
steps.content: Let $w$ be the width of the rectangle in feet. The length is $92$ times the width, so length $=92w$ feet.

steps.title: Confirm using the area formula
steps.content: Area of a rectangle is length times width: $y=(\text{length})(\text{width})=(92w)(w)$. Comparing this to the given $y=(92w)(w)$ confirms that the factor $w$ is the width and $92w$ is the length.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• A) The length of the rectangle, in feet. This equals $92w$ by the relationship stated in the problem.
  • B) The area of the rectangle, in square feet. The area is $y=(92w)(w)$, not $92w$ alone.
  • C) The difference between the length and the width. That would be $92w-w=91w$, not $92w$.
  • D) The width of the rectangle, in feet. The width is $w$, not $92w$. That confirms A is correct.
